name: factor
readme:
Short:        Factor a number (OpenBSD)
Type:         util/misc
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       The Regents of the University of California (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.30

factor - Factor a number (OpenBSD)

Original author: The Regents of the University of California
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  factor [number ...]

  Factors each number into its prime components. With no arguments,
  reads numbers from standard input (one per line).

Examples:
  factor 12            ; outputs: 12: 2 2 3
  factor 100           ; outputs: 100: 2 2 5 5
  factor 17            ; outputs: 17: 17  (prime)
  factor 1 2 3 4 5     ; factor multiple numbers at once

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Multi-file port (factor.c, pattern.c, pr_tbl.c) with pre-computed prime table. 39 tests cover small primes, composites, large numbers, stdin mode, negative numbers, zero, non-numeric input, and multi-argument invocations.
